nodes.x: Praise ye the Lord. I will praise the Lord with my whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and in the congregation.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise the Lord, O my soul.
I will praise the Lord according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the Lord most high.
I will give thee thanks in the great congregation: I will praise thee among much people.
Praise ye the Lord. Sing unto the Lord a new song, and his praise in the congregation of saints.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power.
The works of the Lord are great, sought out of all them that have pleasure therein.
But great and marvelous are the works of the Lord, and the mysteries of his kingdom which he showed unto us, which surpass all understanding in glory, and in might, and in dominion;

But great and marvellous are the works of the Lord and the mysteries of his kingdom which he showed unto us, which surpasses all understanding in glory, and in might, and in dominion, which he commanded us we should not write, while we were yet in the Spirit, and
He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the Lord is gracious and full of compassion.

We are fully aware that in the foregoing remarks we have treated only of the grand introductory principles connected with the kingdom of God, and that there are a great variety of subjects in connexion with the work of the Lord, in the last days, of great interest.
The Lord is good to all: and his tender mercies are over all his works.
His work is honourable and glorious: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
His glory is great in thy salvation: honour and majesty hast thou laid upon him.
The Lord is righteous in all his ways, and holy in all his works.
He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the Lord is gracious and full of compassion.
The Lord is gracious, and full of compassion; slow to anger, and of great mercy.
His work is honourable and glorious: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
Remember his marvellous works that he hath done, his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;
He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the Lord.
Remember his marvellous works that he hath done; his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;
He hath given meat unto them that fear him: he will ever be mindful of his covenant.
The secret of the Lord is with them that fear him; and he will shew them his covenant.
He answereth and saith unto them, He that hath two coats, let him impart to him that hath none; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.
¶ And when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.
And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
And when they had eaten he commanded them that they should break bread, and give unto the multitude.
He hath shewed his people the power of his works, that he may give them the heritage of the heathen.
Declare his glory among the heathen; his marvellous works among all nations.
And he gave them into the hand of the heathen; and they that hated them ruled over them.
And gave them the lands of the heathen: and they inherited the labour of the people;
But were mingled among the heathen, and learned their works.
Declare his glory among the heathen, his wonders among all people.
The works of his hands are verity and judgment; all his commandments are sure.
Now these are the commandments, the statutes, and the judgments, which the Lord your God commanded to teach you, that ye might do them in the land whither ye go to possess it:
Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all things under his feet:
Nevertheless, all flesh is in mine hand, and he that is faithful among you shall not perish by the waters.
They stand fast for ever and ever, and are done in truth and uprightness.
¶ Moreover when ye fast, be not, as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
Moreover, when ye fast be not as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance, for they disfigure their faces that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.
For now we live, if ye stand fast in the Lord.
Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle.
They are upright as the palm tree, but speak not: they must needs be borne, because they cannot go. Be not afraid of them; for they cannot do evil, neither also is it in them to do good.
He sent redemption unto his people: he hath commanded his covenant for ever: holy and reverend is his name.
He hath remembered his covenant for ever, the word which he commanded to a thousand generations.
To him give all the prophets witness, that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.
And he took the book of the covenant, and read in the audience of the people: and they said, All that the Lord hath said will we do, and be obedient.

Hath he commanded any that they should not partake of his salvation? Behold I say unto you, Nay; but he hath given it free for all men; and he hath commanded his people that they should persuade all men to repentance.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.
The fear of the Lord is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility.
And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.
The fear of the Lord tendeth to life: and he that hath it shall abide satisfied; he shall not be visited with evil.

“Fear,” said he, “the Lord, and keep His 
commandments.’ For if you keep the com- 
mandments of God, you will be powerful in 
every action, and every one of your actions will 
be incomparable. For, fearing the Lord, you 
will do all things well. This is the fear which 
you ought to have, that you may be saved. But 
fear not the devil; for, fearing the Lord, you 
will have dominion over the devil,…
